FT_IC1.evidence: States "9855 children from the USA who were part of the ABCD dataset with measures of intelligence at baseline (ages 9-10) and after two years."

FT_IC5.evidence: Original analysis of digital media types (watching, socializing, gaming) on intelligence with genetic and SES controls; longitudinal design with baseline and 2-year follow-up.

FT_IC6.evidence: No evidence of duplication; unique analysis of ABCD cohort with specific focus on screen time types and cognitive outcomes.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: No explicit phone/SM-specific labeled numeric estimates found. Step 2: "Socializing" and "watching" composites lack specificity; "socializing" does not clearly denote phone/SM socializing vs. other digital socializing. Multi-type aggregation without separate phone/SM breakout fails the mechanical requirement. Binding rule: composite content unclear; does not meet phone/SM-specificity standard.

notes: Paper uses "watching," "socializing," and "gaming" as screen time categories but lacks phone/SM-specific operationalization required by IC2 Step 1. Composites are not sufficiently granular.

FT_IC1.evidence: Explicitly states "11,000+ children aged 9-11 from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development cohort" in U.S. context.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis investigating associations between polygenic risk for ADHD, screen time utilization (STU), and white matter microstructure; includes both cross-sectional and longitudinal analyses.

FT_IC6.evidence: No evidence of duplication; unique analysis of ABCD cohort examining genetic, neuroimaging, and behavioral data integration.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: STU (screen time utilization) is not labeled as phone/SM-specific; methods do not restrict to named platforms or phone/SM-specific measures. Step 2: STU is a composite aggregate measure. Binding rule: "Multi-device aggregated time ('computer + tablet + cellphone') fails IC2." STU in this context encompasses generic screen time without platform-specific breakout, meeting the binding failure criterion.

notes: Paper measures generic "screen time utilization" without phone/SM-specific operationalization. Fails binding rule on multi-device aggregation without separate breakout.

FT_IC1.evidence: States "Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) participants with useable baseline structural imaging (N = 10,691)" from U.S. cohort.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original structural neuroimaging analysis using JIVE approach to identify brain structural covariation patterns linked to screen media activity and externalizing behaviors.

FT_IC6.evidence: No evidence of duplication; unique analysis of ABCD baseline structural imaging data with novel covariation pattern approach.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: SMA (screen media activity) is not labeled with phone/SM-specific variables; no named platform or phone/SM-specific numeric estimates found. Step 2: SMA is a composite measure; methods do not specify exclusive phone/SM content vs. other screen types (TV, computer-only, unspecified gaming). Without granular phone/SM-specific items or separate breakout per binding clarifications, fails mechanical requirement.

notes: Paper uses "screen media activity" (SMA) as exposure variable but composite lacks phone/SM-specific operationalization. Other-side outcome (externalizing psychopathology) qualifies as individual-trait, but IC2 composite-content requirement not met.
